reasoning: Confirmed real. The hook 'admin_action_wpcaptcha_install_template' is registered at advanced-google-recaptcha.php line 107 inside is_admin() (line 88), which is NOT a capability check — it means 'we are in the admin area', not 'the user is an administrator'. WordPress fires admin_action_* hooks for any logged-in user who hits wp-admin/admin.php with the matching action= parameter. The handler WPCaptcha_Functions::install_template() (libs/functions.php ~line 1317) calls only check_admin_referer('wpcaptcha_install_template') and has no current_user_can() guard whatsoever. A subscriber-level user can generate their own valid nonce via wp_create_nonce (nonces are per-user, not per-capability) and submit GET wp-admin/admin.php?action=wpcaptcha_install_template&template=<id>&_wpnonce=<own_nonce>. The CSRF check passes, array_key_exists validates the template ID against get_templates(), then array_merge overwrites all design options and update_option(WPCAPTCHA_OPTIONS_KEY, ...) persists the change. The template parameter is sanitize_key() sanitized and validated against the known template list, so there is no injection risk, but the privilege escalation (any auth user changes plugin settings) is real. The claimed reachability (any authenticated user, subscriber+) is correct. No missed guard was found.
